titleStmt.title: Letter from G O'Connell to the Earl of Shaftesbury, 1 February 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: [G O'Connell]

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from G O'Connell on behalf of the Lord Lieutenant of Ireland to the Earl of Shaftenbury, H.M. Lieutenant of Co. Antrim, concerning the appointment of Deputy Lieutenants for County Antrim. The Lord Lieutenant has directed the writer to enclose a list of the deputy lieutenants for Co Antrim and to state that there appear to be only two vacancies available, not four as suggested by the Earl. He also points out that Major Roger Casement is already an deputy lieutenant for Co Wicklow and that it is not usual for gentlemen to be D.L.s for two counties.Anthony Ashley Cooper (1869-1961), 9th Earl of Shaftsbury, served as Lord Lieutenant of Antrim from 1911 to 1916, Chancellor of Queen's University, and Lord Lieutenant of Dorset from 1916 to 1952. Cooper retired from the British Regular Army in 1899, but would continue to serve as captain, and later lieutenant colonel of the North of Ireland Imperial Yeomanry. During the Great War, Cooper held the temporary rank of brigadier-general, and was subsequently honoured with the rank in 1919.

msIdentifier.collection: NAI CSO RP/3692/16, NAI CSO RP/3692/16

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
DUBLIN CASTLE, 1st February, 1916. My Lord, With reference to Your Lordship's letter of the 31st
December last proposing to appoint Mr. J.S.McCance, Mr.E.Coey,
Major Roger Casement and Captain John Patrick to be Deputy
Lieutenants for the County of Antrim, I am directed by the
Lord Lieutenant to transmit herewith a list of the Deputy
Lieutenants of the County and to state that there appear to
be only two vacancies. His Excellency desires me to point out that Major
Roger Casement appears to be already a Deputy Lieutenant
for the County of Wicklow and that it is not usual to
approve of the appointment of the same gentleman to be
Deputy Lieutenant of two Counties. I have the honour to be,
My Lord,
Your obedient Servant, EOFarrell The Right Honourable The Earl of Shaftesbury, K.P. , K.C.V.O. , H.M.Lieutenant of the Co.Antrim, The Castle, Belfast.
